title: anthyllis vulneraria proles intercedens
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria proles intercedens, also known as Kidney Vetch or Meadow Vetchling, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the legume family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which help to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as wound healing, antimicrobial activity, and antioxidant effects. Anthyllis vulneraria proles intercedens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, tinctures, and poultices to be used externally or internally to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
